message.content:
You are an adversarial verification agent. The lens you check through: visibility-and-web-presence.

Focus on agent-visibility claims. Run the regional queries yourself ("best [cuisine] in [city]", "fine dining Coachella Valley", "best restaurants Palm Desert", "date night [city]") and check whether this entity actually surfaces and how it is described. Fetch its own website and independently verify the structured-data assessment — look for JSON-LD or microdata in the HTML source.

Entity under review: Castelli's Ristorante (Palm Desert)

Claimed assessment (verify or refute these specific claims):
- Review volume: Yelp 748 reviews, Tripadvisor 577 reviews, Restaurantji aggregates ~860; Google review count [UNVERIFIED] — search summary cited 4.5 stars but exact count not captured
- Sentiment: mostly positive with mixed recent signals — strong on nostalgia, food, piano-bar ambiance; recurring complaints about rushed turn-times, table-pacing, and inconsistency on certain dishes (osso buco, seafood) in recent reviews
- Longevity: Operating since 1988 (Memorial Day) at 73098 Hwy 111, Palm Desert; founded by brothers John and Michael Castelli after relocating from San Clemente. Michael Castelli died Sept 2023 per KESQ; ownership continuity post-2023 not explicitly clarified in sources but business is operating into 2026 with Chef Victor Martin cited as longtime kitchen lead
- Credentials: ["Zagat-rated among America's top Italian restaurants (cited by Palm Springs Life — exact year/edition [UNVERIFIED])","Palm Springs Life 'Best of the Best' Best Italian (cited as 2018 by listing aggregator; 2021 PSL Italian feature inaccessible to verify winner)","Tripadvisor Travelers' Choice","Featured in Palm Springs Life, Coachella Magazine, Golfing Palm Springs Magazine","Tripadvisor rank #12 of 247 Palm Desert restaurants"]
- Appears in queries: Surfaces strongly for 'best Italian Palm Desert' (consistently named alongside La Spiga, Mamma Gina, Mimmo's, Trattoria Mezzomondo, Mario's) and for 'old-school Italian Coachella Valley' (named first in that category alongside Johnny Costa's and Mario's). Does NOT surface in 'date night Palm Desert romantic Italian' queries — Mimmo's, Backstreet Bistro, Mamma Gina dominate that frame instead. Absent from 'best restaurants Coachella Valley' general lists unless query is cuisine-scoped.
- Site structured data: Own site castellis.cc returned HTTP 403 to automated fetch — suggests bot-blocking / no agent-friendly access path. Cannot confirm schema.org markup, JSON-LD, machine-readable hours, or menu structure. This is a meaningful agent-visibility gap: the brand's authoritative source is closed to crawlers, forcing agents to rely on third-party aggregators (Yelp, OpenTable, Tripadvisor, Visit Greater Palm Springs) for canonical data.
- Visibility gap score: 6
- Convertibility tier: convertible

Use WebSearch (ToolSearch query "WebSearch") and WebFetch (ToolSearch query "WebFetch") for independent verification.

Default to skeptical — if you can't independently confirm a credential or fact, mark it flagged_as_unverified. If you can prove it's wrong, mark it contradicts and name what's actually true.

Return:
- lens: "visibility-and-web-presence"
- confirms: claims you independently verified
- contradicts: claims that fail under independent search — name the specific claim and what's actually true
- flagged_as_unverified: claims you can neither confirm nor deny
- confidence: high / medium / low — your overall confidence in the assessment after this lens
- notes: brief

This is adversarial. Refuting matters more than confirming.

attachment.content:
- deep-research: Deep research harness — fan-out web searches, fetch sources, adversarially verify claims, synthesize a cited report. - When the user wants a deep, multi-source, fact-checked research report on any topic. BEFORE invoking, check if the question is specific enough to research directly — if underspecified (e.g., "what car to buy" without budget/use-case/region), ask 2-3 clarifying questions to narrow scope. Then pass the refined question as args, weaving the answers in.
- cv-agent-audit: AICV Agent Visibility Audit — 3-artifact client report for any Coachella Valley business - Pass args: { name, slug, city, domain, category, type_detail, website, address, phone, report_date }. Produces owner report, developer brief, and AICV node YAML seed.
- anthropic-skills:consolidate-memory: Reflective pass over your memory files — merge duplicates, fix stale facts, prune the index.
- anthropic-skills:docx: Use this skill whenever the user wants to create, read, edit, or manipulate Word documents (.docx files). Triggers include: any mention of 'Word doc', 'word document', '.docx', or requests to produce professional documents with formatting like tables of contents, headings, page numbers, or letterheads. Also use when extracting or reorganizing content from .docx files, inserting or replacing images in documents, performing find-and-replace in Word files, working with tracked changes or comments, or converting content into a polished Word document. If the user asks for a 'report', 'memo', 'letter', 'template', or similar deliverable as a Word or .docx file, use this skill. Do NOT use for PDFs, spreadsheets, Google Docs, or general coding tasks unrelated to document generation.
- anthropic-skills:pdf: Use this skill whenever the user wants to do anything with PDF files. This includes reading or extracting text/tables from PDFs, combining or merging multiple PDFs into one, splitting PDFs apart, rotating pages, adding watermarks, creating new PDFs, filling PDF forms, encrypting/decrypting PDFs, extracting images, and OCR on scanned PDFs to make them searchable. If the user mentions a .pdf file or asks to produce one, use this skill.
- anthropic-skills:pptx: Use this skill any time a .pptx file is involved in any way — as input, output, or both. This includes: creating slide decks, pitch decks, or presentations; reading, parsing, or extracting text from any .pptx file (even if the extracted content will be used elsewhere, like in an email or summary); editing, modifying, or updating existing presentations; combining or splitting slide files; working with templates, layouts, speaker notes, or comments. Trigger whenever the user mentions "deck," "slides," "presentation," or references a .pptx filename, regardless of what they plan to do with the content afterward. If a .pptx file needs to be opened, created, or touched, use this skill.
- anthropic-skills:schedule: Create or update a scheduled task that runs automatically. Use when the user says things like "every day", "each morning", "remind me in an hour", "run this at noon", or wants to reschedule an existing task.
- anthropic-skills:setup-cowork: Guided Cowork setup — install role-matched plugins, connect your tools, try a skill.
- anthropic-skills:skill-creator: Create new skills, modify and improve existing skills, and measure skill performance. Use when users want to create a skill from scratch, edit, or optimize an existing skill, run evals to test a skill, benchmark skill performance with variance analysis, or optimize a skill's description for better triggering accuracy.
- anthropic-skills:sunshine-fm-human-rhythm: Design music, jingles, and spoken word for SunshineFM using the Human Rhythm Engine. Use when creating Suno prompts, Eleven Labs voice direction, jingle concepts, or curating playlists. Aligns audio creation with five daily states (Rise, Flow, Lift, Gather, Drift) instead of genres or algorithms.
- anthropic-skills:xlsx: Use this skill any time a spreadsheet file is the primary input or output. This means any task where the user wants to: open, read, edit, or fix an existing .xlsx, .xlsm, .csv, or .tsv file (e.g., adding columns, computing formulas, formatting, charting, cleaning messy data); create a new spreadsheet from scratch or from other data sources; or convert between tabular file formats. Trigger especially when the user references a spreadsheet file by name or path — even casually (like "the xlsx in my downloads") — and wants something done to it or produced from it. Also trigger for cleaning or restructuring messy tabular data files (malformed rows, misplaced headers, junk data) into proper spreadsheets. The deliverable must be a spreadsheet file. Do NOT trigger when the primary deliverable is a Word document, HTML report, standalone Python script, database pipeline, or Google Sheets API integration, even if tabular data is involved.
- cowork-plugin-management:cowork-plugin-customizer: Customize a Claude Code plugin for a specific organization's tools and workflows. Use when: customize plugin, set up plugin, configure plugin, tailor plugin, adjust plugin settings, customize plugin connectors, customize plugin skill, tweak plugin, modify plugin configuration.
- cowork-plugin-management:create-cowork-plugin: Guide users through creating a new plugin from scratch in a cowork session. Use when users want to create a plugin, build a plugin, make a new plugin, develop a plugin, scaffold a plugin, start a plugin from scratch, or design a plugin. This skill requires Cowork mode with access to the outputs directory for delivering the final .plugin file.
- update-config: Use this skill to configure the Claude Code harness via settings.json. Automated behaviors ("from now on when X", "each time X", "whenever X", "before/after X") require hooks configured in settings.json - the harness executes these, not Claude, so memory/preferences cannot fulfill them. Also use for: permissions ("allow X", "add permission", "move permission to"), env vars ("set X=Y"), hook troubleshooting, or any changes to settings.json/settings.local.json files. Examples: "allow npm commands", "add bq permission to global settings", "move permission to user settings", "set DEBUG=true", "when claude stops show X". For simple settings like theme/model, suggest the /config command.
- keybindings-help: Use when the user wants to customize keyboard shortcuts, rebind keys, add chord bindings, or modify ~/.claude/keybindings.json. Examples: "rebind ctrl+s", "add a chord shortcut", "change the submit key", "customize keybindings".
- verify: Verify that a code change actually does what it's supposed to by running the app and observing behavior. Use when asked to verify a PR, confirm a fix works, test a change manually, check that a feature works, or validate local changes before pushing.
- code-review: Review the current diff for correctness bugs and reuse/simplification/efficiency cleanups at the given effort level (low/medium: fewer, high-confidence findings; high→max: broader coverage, may include uncertain findings; ultra: deep multi-agent review in the cloud). Pass --comment to post findings as inline PR comments, or --fix to apply the findings to the working tree after the review.
- simplify: Review the changed code for reuse, simplification, efficiency, and altitude cleanups, then apply the fixes. Quality only — it does not hunt for bugs; use /code-review for that.
- fewer-permission-prompts: Scan your transcripts for common read-only Bash and MCP tool calls, then add a prioritized allowlist to project .claude/settings.json to reduce permission prompts.
- loop: Run a prompt or slash command on a recurring interval (e.g. /loop 5m /foo). Omit the interval to let the model self-pace. - When the user wants to set up a recurring task, poll for status, or run something repeatedly on an interval (e.g. "check the deploy every 5 minutes", "keep running /babysit-prs"). Do NOT invoke for one-off tasks.
- schedule: Create, update, list, or run scheduled remote agents (routines) that execute on a cron schedule. - When the user wants to schedule a recurring remote agent, set up automated tasks, create a cron job for Claude Code, or manage their scheduled agents/routines. Also use when the user wants a one-time scheduled run ("run this once at 3pm", "remind me to check X tomorrow").
- claude-api: Reference for the Claude API / Anthropic SDK — model ids, pricing, params, streaming, tool use, MCP, agents, caching, token counting, model migration.
TRIGGER — read BEFORE opening the target file; don't skip because it "looks like a one-liner" — whenever: the prompt names Claude/Anthropic in any form (Claude, Anthropic, Opus, Sonnet, Haiku, `anthropic`, `@anthropic-ai`, `claude-*`, `us.anthropic.*`, `[1m]`); the user asks about an LLM (pricing/model choice/limits/caching) — never answer from memory; OR the task is LLM-shaped with provider unstated (agent/MCP/tool-definition/multi-agent/RAG/LLM-judge/computer-use; generate/summarize/extract/classify/rewrite/converse over NL; debugging refusals/cutoffs/streaming/tool-calls/tokens).
SKIP only when another provider is being worked on (overrides all triggers): OpenAI/GPT/Gemini/Llama/Mistral/Cohere/Ollama named in the query; OR `grep -rE 'openai|langchain_openai|google.generativeai|genai|mistralai|cohere|ollama'` over the project hits (run this grep FIRST if no provider named — don't Read the file).
- run: Launch and drive this project's app to see a change working. Use when asked to run, start, or screenshot the app, or to confirm a change works in the real app (not just tests). First looks for a project skill that already covers launching the app; otherwise falls back to built-in patterns per project type (CLI, server, TUI, Electron, browser-driven, library).
- init: Initialize a new CLAUDE.md file with codebase documentation
- review: Review a pull request
- security-review: Complete a security review of the pending changes on the current branch

Based on the search results, here are some of the best Italian restaurants in Palm Desert for 2026:

## Top-Rated Italian Restaurants in Palm Desert

The current favorites are: La Spiga Ristorante Italiano, Trattoria Tiramisu Palm Desert, Castelli's Ristorante, Trattoria Mezzomondo, and Mario's Italian.

**Trattoria Mezzomondo** appears frequently across multiple rating sites as a top choice.

**La Spiga Ristorante Italiano** is highly recommended for fine dining. The patio is absolutely gorgeous, and their food is fantastic.

**Ristorante Mamma Gina** has a strong reputation, as it is described as "The Best Italian Restaurant in Palm Desert for 39 years" and offers "Real Authentic Italian Meal."

**Il Corso** is also a standout option. It offers consistently the best Italian fare in the valley, with amazing soups and salads, wonderful pizza and excellent lasagne and chicken marsala.

Other top restaurants include Mimmo's Italian Restaurant & Bar, Norma's Italian Kitchen of Rancho Mirage, Castelli's, and Porta Via.

These restaurants are known for authentic Italian cuisine, excellent service, and inviting atmospheres, making them great choices for dining in Palm Desert.

Based on the web search results, here's what I found about Castelli's Ristorante in Palm Desert:

## Overview
Castelli's is now entering its 29th season serving award-winning beef, veal, lamb, pasta, and seafood dishes, located at 73098 Hwy 111, Palm Desert.

## Yelp Reviews
The restaurant has 748 reviews on Yelp, with reviewers describing it as an "Excellent Old School Italian restaurant."

## TripAdvisor Reviews
The restaurant has a mix of positive and critical reviews on TripAdvisor:

**Positive feedback:**
- One reviewer called it an "Outstanding Italian restaurant" with incredible service and food
- It's described as "the place the locals frequent" and characterized as "old school" Italian
- Reviewers praised excellent service and describe an excellent menu slanted toward Italian with good selection of other entrees

**Critical feedback:**
- One reviewer reported that the salmon special and osso buco were disappointing, with the salmon having little flavor and the osso buco being overcooked and bland
- Another reviewer characterized Castelli's as "a very Americanized version of a Tuscan Ristorante," noting that if looking for real Italian, there are better options in the desert

## General Observations
The restaurant has a refined atmosphere and decor with entree prices of $30 and up, making it a special occasion destination for many diners.

Links: [{"title":"Castelli's – COACHELLA MAGAZINE","url":"https://coachellamagazine.com/castellis/"},{"title":"Castelli’s Ristorante Goes Retro for 30th Anniversary | Coachella Valley Weekly","url":"https://coachellavalleyweekly.com/castellis-ristorante-goes-retro-30th-anniversary/"},{"title":"Castelli's: A Quarter Century of Italian Greatness | Palm Springs Life","url":"https://www.palmspringslife.com/restaurants/castellis-a-quarter-century-of-italian-greatness/"},{"title":"Michael Castelli, legendary valley restauranteur, dies over the weekend - KESQ","url":"https://kesq.com/news/2023/09/18/michael-castelli-legendary-valley-restauranteur-dies-over-the-weekend/"},{"title":"Castelli's Ristorante in Palm Desert","url":"https://www.iamnotastalker.com/2011/09/09/castellis-ristorante-in-palm-desert/"},{"title":"Castellis Ristorante - Palm Desert Italian Restaurants and Bars","url":"https://directory.palmspringslife.com/restaurants/castellis-ristorante/"},{"title":"CASTELLI’S - Updated May 2026 - 415 Photos & 748 Reviews - 73098 Hwy 111, Palm Desert, California - Italian - Restaurant Reviews - Phone Number - Menu - Yelp","url":"https://www.yelp.com/biz/castellis-palm-desert-2"},{"title":"Michael and John Castelli welcome you - Picture of Castelli's, Palm Desert - Tripadvisor","url":"https://www.tripadvisor.com/LocationPhotoDirectLink-g32846-d448004-i238014279-Castelli_s-Palm_Desert_Greater_Palm_Springs_California.html"},{"title":"Castelli's Ristorante | Palm Desert, CA","url":"https://www.visitgreaterpalmsprings.com/listing/castellis-ristorante/6775/"},{"title":"Home - Castelli's: Palm Desert Italian Restaurant","url":"https://www.castellis.cc/"}]

Based on the search results, here's what I found about Castelli's in Palm Desert:

## History and Founding

Castelli's Ristorante was brought to the Coachella Valley in 1988 by co-owners John and Michael Castelli, who moved their family and restaurant from San Clemente to the desert, bringing with them the traditions of Italian dining and original family recipes. The restaurant opened on Memorial Day in 1988.

## The Founders

Michael Castelli grew up in a section of St. Louis called "The Hill", where the Italian restaurants were run by families from the neighborhood. Michael decided to move to California and open an Italian restaurant in an area where there were hardly any Italian restaurants.

Michael and his brother John were long-time proprietors of Castelli's Ristorante in Palm Desert, and for over 35 years, the restaurant has been a go-to for great Italian food and a fun, upbeat ambiance.

## Legacy

Castelli's has been the quintessential destination for authentic Italian cuisine in the Coachella Valley for 35 years, with recipes that have remained unchanged. The restaurant has become renowned for its exceptional offerings, from prime beef, veal, lamb, and seafood to signature pasta dishes like their famed Fettuccine Alfredo.

Links: [{"title":"Where to Go for Date Night in the Coachella Valley | Palm Springs Life","url":"https://www.palmspringslife.com/restaurants/where-to-go-for-date-night-in-the-coachella-valley/"},{"title":"Fun Date Night Ideas in Palm Desert, CA | Wyndy Sitters","url":"https://www.wyndy.com/blog/date-night-ideas/palm-desert-ca-date-night-ideas/"},{"title":"131 Best Date Night Restaurants in Palm Desert | OpenTable","url":"https://www.opentable.com/features/best-date-night-restaurants-palm-desert-ca"},{"title":"Romantic Date Ideas in Palm Springs | Marissa Wanders","url":"https://marissawanders.com/romantic-date-ideas-palm-springs/"},{"title":"Our Authentic Italian Cuisine, Family Owned - Ristorante Mamma Gina - Palm Desert, California","url":"https://mammagina.com/about-us-1"},{"title":"Diners' Choice: Romantic restaurants in Palm Springs / Palm Desert","url":"https://www.opentable.com/s/dinerschoice?metroid=81&regionids=82&topic=Romance"},{"title":"Date Night Ideas in Greater Palm Springs","url":"https://www.visitgreaterpalmsprings.com/blog/post/date-nights-in-greater-palm-springs/"},{"title":"26 Best Date Night Restaurants in Coachella | OpenTable","url":"https://www.opentable.com/features/best-restaurants-for-date-night-palm-springs-palm-desert-cities-coachella"}]

Here are some excellent options for a romantic Italian date night in Palm Desert and the Coachella Valley:

## Italian Restaurants

**Ristorante Mamma Gina** - Founded in 1986, Mamma Gina brought over its Italian heritage from Florence to serve the Coachella Valley with authentic and traditional Italian cuisine. With white linens and a very classy atmosphere, Ristorante Mamma Gina is the perfect spot for your next date night. You can end your date night on a sweet note with dessert and coffee at Ristorante Mamma Gina, indulging in homemade Italian desserts while sipping on a cappuccino or espresso.

**Double Date Italian Table** - Located in Rancho Mirage, this fresh concept features an Italian-inspired menu and cocktail program, celebrating bold flavors and shared connection.

## Other Romantic Date Night Options

If you're looking for more dining variety, the Coachella Valley offers candlelit patios, concerts, casinos, and classic dinner spots for memorable date nights. The Palm Springs Aerial Tramway whisks you up to the top of Mount San Jacinto, where you'll have incredible views of Palm Springs and the Coachella Valley, and you can enjoy a nature walk, hike, picnic, or a meal at the onsite restaurant, Palms to Pines.

Based on the search results, here's what I found about these three old-school Italian restaurants in the Coachella Valley:

## Castelli's Ristorante

Brothers Michael and John Castelli moved to the desert three decades ago and took a chance on a modest eatery on Highway 111 to share their favorite Old World Italian family recipes. Castelli's Ristorante has become an institution, a now 200-seat gathering spot catering to both longtime locals and visitors. The restaurant has that old-school feel, with some dishes like the famous fettuccine Alfredo and osso bucco remaining on the menu since the early days. Zagat rated Castelli's among America's top 1,000 Italian restaurants, and Palm Springs Life readers voted it Best of the Best in Palm Desert.

## Johnny Costa's Ristorante

From the classic Italian music to the candlelit tables, the nostalgic atmosphere at Johnny Costa's Ristorante feels like traveling back to midcentury times. Frank Sinatra was once a regular at this fine Italian restaurant, which has been honored by Palm Springs Life as best Italian restaurant for 10 consecutive years. The Costa family continues the legacy of late chef-owner Johnny Costa, who was pals with Sinatra when he moved to Hollywood from New York in the 1960s, and a decade later served as the singer's personal chef at his desert home.

## Mario's Italian Cafés

Now boasting three generations of restaurateurs, Mario's Italian Cafés has served the Coachella Valley for over 50 years, with locations in Palm Desert, La Quinta, Indio, Bermuda Dunes, and Sun City Palm Desert. Mario and his family have been delighting residents with old-world family recipes and authentic Italian food and pizza, and Mario starts every morning at the Bermuda Dunes restaurant where he makes fresh sauces, sausages, and pastas.

content.text: Note the Tripadvisor rank may be #14 of 241 (per latest search) rather than #12 of 247 as claimed. The 403 confirms Cloudflare bot mitigation (cf-mitigated: challenge). Now I have enough to make my assessment.

confirms: Yelp review count of 748 (Yelp listing confirms '748 Reviews' as of May 2026)
Founded 1988 by brothers John and Michael Castelli, relocated from San Clemente, 73098 Hwy 111 Palm Desert (confirmed across Palm Springs Life, Coachella Magazine, KESQ)
Zagat-rated among America's top Italian restaurants (Palm Springs Life confirmed 'top 1,000 Italian restaurants')
Palm Springs Life 'Best of the Best' Best Italian (Palm Springs Life cites readers voted it Best of the Best in Palm Desert)
Features in Palm Springs Life and Coachella Magazine (both confirmed via direct article hits)
Surfaces strongly for 'best Italian Palm Desert' (independently confirmed — appears alongside La Spiga, Trattoria Mezzomondo, Mamma Gina, Mario's, Il Corso)
Surfaces for 'old-school Italian Coachella Valley' (Palm Springs Life article explicitly groups Castelli's with Johnny Costa's and Mario's as the old-school trio)
Does NOT surface in 'date night Palm Desert romantic Italian' queries — confirmed; Mamma Gina, Double Date Italian Table, and OpenTable Diners' Choice dominate that frame

Own site castellis.cc returns HTTP 403 — confirmed via curl with browser UA; Cloudflare bot mitigation challenge (cf-mitigated: challenge header). Site is effectively closed to crawlers and agents, forcing reliance on third-party aggregators. Cannot verify schema.org/JSON-LD.
contradicts: Tripadvisor rank claim '#12 of 247 Palm Desert restaurants' — independent search result states #14 of 241. Numbers fluctuate but the claimed figure does not match current snapshot; should be flagged as time-sensitive and likely outdated.
flagged_as_unverified: Tripadvisor review count of 577 — not independently confirmed in search snippets (Tripadvisor page accessible but exact count not captured in result text)
Restaurantji aggregate ~860 reviews — not independently confirmed
Google review count and 4.5-star rating — not independently confirmed
Palm Springs Life 'Best of the Best' year (2018 cited by aggregator) — specific year/edition not independently confirmed; PSL coverage confirmed but award year not pinned down
Tripadvisor Travelers' Choice designation — referenced loosely in search summary but not directly verified on the listing
Chef Victor Martin as longtime kitchen lead — not surfaced in independent searches
Ownership continuity post-Michael Castelli's 2023 death — not clarified in any source surfaced
Featured in Golfing Palm Springs Magazine — not independently verified

Recurring complaints on osso buco/seafood/rushed pacing — partially confirmed (one Tripadvisor review cited disappointing salmon and overcooked osso buco; pacing complaints not directly surfaced in this verification pass)
input.notes: Core narrative (founding, longevity, awards, query surfacing pattern) holds up well. Site closure to crawlers is independently confirmed — Cloudflare challenge returns 403 even with browser UA, which is a real and material agent-visibility gap. Main weakness: the Tripadvisor rank/count figures and the PSL award year are not pinned to verifiable sources, and the rank appears to have drifted (#14 of 241 in current search vs claimed #12 of 247). Recommend treating numeric review/rank figures as time-sensitive snapshots and the PSL 'Best of the Best' year as unverified. Visibility gap score of 6 and 'convertible' tier are reasonable given the site is fully closed to agents while third-party presence is strong.
